response.justification: The candidate comfortably meets the minimum bar of 15+ years of software engineering experience and a technical bachelor's degree, with genuine breadth across web, embedded, low-latency, and geospatial systems, plus entrepreneurial credibility as the long-term founder of an open-source GIS platform. However, his AI exposure is recent and narrow (MCP code generators and agentic tooling at Google since 2025), he has no advanced degree in an AI-related field, and there is no evidence of recognized AI thought leadership, shipped AI products, or director-level strategic influence — all central to this principal AI-strategy role. He reads as a strong senior/staff builder rather than a director-level AI strategist.
strengths: 19+ years of hands-on engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput low-latency systems
Current Google staff engineer working on AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic workflow tooling
Entrepreneurial track record: founded and sustained an open-source geo-data platform used by NGOs, plus conference talks and workshops
concerns: No recognized AI/ML expertise, advanced AI degree, or demonstrated AI products at platform scale
Career trajectory tops out at senior/staff IC; no evidence of director-level strategic scope or advising executive customers
Resume inconsistencies (e.g., open-source founder role labeled 'Amazon') and public speaking limited to niche developer communities rather than industry-level influence

key_factors.factor: Meets 15-year experience minimum with broad systems breadth
Seniority gap to director-level strategy scope
